final_prompt.prompt:
Use case: illustration-concept-art
Asset type: cyberpunk cityscape illustration, wide cinematic crop
Primary request: a dense, neon-drenched street scene in Neo-Tokyo, year 2077, at night — multicultural chaos, deep perspective from foreground street stalls to skyscrapers dissolving in smog
Scene/backdrop: narrow, overcrowded alley-street flanked by towering buildings plastered with multilingual neon signs (Japanese, Chinese, English); ground-level puddles mirror all neon light; smog-hazed skyline in the far distance
Subject: crowd of pedestrians — a Japanese woman in traditional kimono with high-tech cybernetic prosthetics; augmented laborers in worn work gear with visible body modifications; holographic virtual idol performing mid-air above the street; delivery drones weaving through low airspace; floating ad billboards cycling product commercials
Style/medium: digital illustration, painterly concept art, Blade Runner 2049 visual aesthetic — saturated color, dense layered detail, dystopian-futuristic atmosphere
Composition/framing: deep perspective, extreme depth of field — near foreground street vendors → mid-ground crowd → far background skyscraper cluster vanishing into haze; slight low-angle street-level view
Lighting/mood: neon-as-daylight intensity; dominant pink, cyan-blue, and amber-orange light sources; wet reflections on pavement creating mirror-like puddle effects; moody, oppressive yet visually overwhelming
Color palette: hot pink neon + electric cyan + amber-orange + deep shadow blacks + smog grey
Materials/textures: wet asphalt with neon reflections, worn fabric and exposed metal cybernetics, translucent holographic light surfaces, grimy concrete and rusted signage frames
Constraints: preserve all three languages on signage (Japanese / Chinese / English); keep holographic idol visually distinct from physical crowd; maintain extreme scene density and layering throughout
Avoid: clean utopian aesthetic, sparse composition, pastel or desaturated tones, generic sci-fi clichés without cultural specificity
